headline: GOLD CRASHES TO $4,557, One-Month Low as Iran Stalemate and Hormuz Closure Fan Inflation Fears
snippet: Spot gold tumbled nearly 2% to $4,557/oz on Wednesday, its weakest since late March, as stalled US-Iran peace negotiations and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz amplify inflation concerns. Markets are increasingly pricing in a longer rate-hold by central banks, weighing on non-yielding bullion despite gold still sitting $1,268 above year-ago levels.

headline: CHINA'S CMOC WINS Ecuador's Largest Gold Mine: $1.7B Los Cangrejos Deal Gives Beijing New Latin American Foothold
snippet: Ecuador signed a $1.7 billion mining contract with CMOC Group's local subsidiary ODIN Mining del Ecuador for the Los Cangrejos gold-copper project in El Oro province, the country's largest undeveloped gold deposit. The state retains 50% of project value including $54M in advance royalties, with construction set to begin before end of 2027 and projected state revenues of $4.39 billion over the mine's life.

headline: RARE EARTHS AMERICAS Launches $368M IPO on NYSE as Sector Supply-Chain Urgency Builds
snippet: Rare Earths Americas Inc. (ticker: REA) officially launched its IPO roadshow on April 28, targeting a valuation of up to $368.4 million with 2.78 million shares priced at $17–$19 each on NYSE American. The company, led by underwriters Cantor and Stifel, plans to raise up to $52.8 million in an offering timed to growing investor appetite for China-independent rare earth supply chains.

headline: Mining M&A HITS $21.6B in Q1 2026 — Strongest Quarter Since 2023, Critical Minerals Drive Surge
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion in Q1 2026, a 34% jump from Q1 2025 and the highest first-quarter total since 2023, according to a White & Case report published today. Deal volume also climbed to 121 transactions, with supply-chain security for critical minerals now overtaking price as the primary deal driver.

headline: VALE Q1 PROFIT JUMPS 36% to $1.89B as Base Metals EBITDA More Than Doubles
snippet: Brazil's Vale posted a 36% year-over-year rise in Q1 net profit to $1.89 billion on April 28, driven by its highest iron ore sales for a first quarter since 2018 and sharply higher base metals earnings across copper and nickel. Adjusted EBITDA came in at $3.83 billion, up 23%, though both figures missed analyst consensus as the company flagged ongoing cost pressures.

headline: UNREGULATED RARE EARTH MINES in Myanmar Spreading Into Laos, Poisoning Mekong Tributaries With Arsenic and Mercury
snippet: A rising demand for rare earth materials is fueling an unregulated mining boom centered in conflict-torn Myanmar that is now spreading into Laos, with water, fish, and sediment samples from Mekong tributaries showing dangerous levels of arsenic, mercury, lead, and cadmium. The findings raise fresh questions about the true environmental cost of the West's push to build China-independent rare earth supply chains.

headline: PRIVATE EQUITY FLOODS Mining With Record $9B in Q1 as Glencore's DRC Copper-Cobalt Assets Dominate
snippet: Private equity and venture capital investment in metals and mining surged to $9.09 billion in Q1 2026, smashing prior annual totals, with the $9 billion Orion Resource Partners / DFC acquisition of a 40% stake in Glencore's Mutanda copper-cobalt assets in the DRC accounting for virtually the entire haul. PE now represents 22.5% of mining deal activity, up from 1.7% in full-year 2025.

headline: REPORT: U.S. MINT Buying Gold Sourced From Cartel-Controlled Mines, New York Times Investigation Finds
snippet: A New York Times investigation cited by Money Metals Exchange reveals that the U.S. Mint has been purchasing an untold amount of gold sourced from mines operating under cartel control, raising serious questions about the integrity of American sovereign coin supply chains. The report adds to growing scrutiny of informal gold supply networks operating outside LBMA and OECD due-diligence frameworks.

headline: Agnico Eagle & Endeavour Silver Named TOP GOLD-SILVER PICKS as Mining Equities Finally Play Catch-Up
snippet: With gold above $4,500 and silver above $70, Motley Fool Canada highlights Agnico Eagle — which generated US$4.4 billion in free cash flow in 2025 and is targeting 20–30% production growth by the early 2030s — as the top risk-adjusted pick, while Endeavour Silver offers a higher-upside bet with a brand-new mine ramping and one of the world's largest undeveloped silver deposits advancing toward a construction decision.

headline: KODIAK COPPER Boots 6,500m Drill Program at BC Porphyry With 36 Targets; AI-Assisted Targeting Adds New Prospects
snippet: Kodiak Copper Corp. (TSXV: KDK) commenced its fully-funded 2026 exploration program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern BC on April 28, with a 6,500-metre drill campaign targeting all seven deposits in the current mineral resource estimate plus 36 high-priority exploration targets — including new prospects identified using VRIFY AI-assisted targeting across recently acquired claims.

headline: SILVER DEMAND STILL ROBUST: China Imports 790+ Tons Early 2026 Even as Price Drops 3% on Iran-Inflation Fears
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT analysis notes that Chinese silver imports surpassed 790 tonnes in early 2026 — partly driven by solar panel manufacturing — even as spot silver slid over 3% to $73/oz this week on Strait of Hormuz inflation fears. The structural demand case remains intact, with the silver market recording a supply deficit for the fifth consecutive year and Bank of America maintaining a $135–$309/oz 2026 price target.
